sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE caused the character to rotate! Looking at the image, the character is now rotated 90 degrees clockwise - the red blocks are now on the left side instead of the bottom. This is a rotation mechanic.
Let me check the segmentation to understand the new orientation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE - character rotated
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(r for r,c in b)
        maxr = max(r for r,c in b)
        minc = min(c for r,c in b)
        maxc = max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({minr},{maxr},{minc},{maxc}) children={n['children']}")

# Check ASCII to see the rotated character
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Character area ===")
for i in range(20, 50):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][24:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! The character rotated 90 degrees clockwise. The red blocks are now on the left side of the character (like "hands" instead of "feet"). The character is now horizontal instead of vertical.
Looking at the ASCII:
- The white character is now at rows 23-37, cols 32-46 (rotated)
- Red blocks (id=4,8) are at rows 26-28 and 32-34, cols 29-31 (on the left side)
- The green rectangle (N) is split into two parts: id=6 (18 pixels) and id=7 (108 pixels)
- There's still a red block (id=10) at rows 41-43, cols 38-40
The character seems to be "stamping" onto the green rectangle - parts of the green rectangle are being filled with white (the character's color). I can see "WWW" appearing inside the green rectangle area.
The goal might be to rotate and position the character so that when it overlaps with the green rectangle, it fills the hole or completes the shape.
Let me think about this differently. The green rectangle has a hole (sky blue area). The character has a specific shape. Maybe the goal is to position the character so its shape matches the hole in the green rectangle.
Looking at the hole in the green rectangle: it's at rows 32-46, cols 44-46 (approximately). The character's shape when rotated might fit into this hole.
Let me try rotating again and see what happens.

</parameter>
